lines: Find a comfortable position. [long pause] Sitting is ideal for this one, but whatever works. [long pause] Take a moment to adjust. [long pause] Settle in.

Close your eyes. [long pause] Take a slow breath in through your nose... [long pause] [long pause] two, three, four... [long pause] [long pause] and out through your mouth... [long pause] [long pause] two, three, four, five, six. [long pause] A long exhale. [long pause] Releasing.
Again. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] two, three, four... [long pause] [long pause] and out... [long pause] [long pause] two, three, four, five, six.
One more. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] and a long, slow release.
Let the breathing go natural now.
We're going to begin with your body. [long pause] Because today's meditation is about something your body already knows — the difference between gripping and releasing.
Start with your jaw. [long pause] Is it clenched? [long pause] Most people carry tension there without realizing it. [long pause] Let it drop. [long pause] Let your lips part just slightly.
Your shoulders. [long pause] Are they lifted? [long pause] Braced? [long pause] Like you're expecting something to land on you? [long pause] Let them fall. [long pause] All the way down.

Your hands. [long pause] Where are they? [long pause] And what shape are they in? [long pause] Are they fisted? [long pause] Curled? [long pause] Gripping the armrest, your own clothing, your own skin?

Now... [long pause] [long pause] make a fist. [long pause] Both hands. [long pause] Deliberately. [long pause] Squeeze. [long pause] Feel what that does to your whole body. [long pause] Notice how the tension radiates — up your forearms, into your shoulders, into your jaw, your chest. [long pause] Everything tightens when you grip.

This is how many of us move through life. [long pause] Gripping. [long pause] Holding on. [long pause] Trying to control the outcome, manage the situation, prevent the thing we're afraid of. [long pause] It's exhausting. [long pause] And it doesn't work.
Keep your fists closed for another moment. [long pause] And ask yourself: what am I gripping right now? [long pause] Not with my hands — with my life. [long pause] What am I trying to control?

Is it a relationship? [long pause] An outcome at work? [long pause] Your health? [long pause] Your children's choices? [long pause] Someone's opinion of you? [long pause] The future? [long pause] A timeline you set for yourself?
Name another one if it comes.

Notice what each of those things does to your body when you think about them. [long pause] The tightening. [long pause] The bracing. [long pause] The clenching. [long pause] Control lives in the body as tension. [long pause] It masquerades as responsibility, but it feels like exhaustion.

Now... [long pause] [long pause] very slowly... [long pause] [long pause] begin to open your hands. [long pause] Not all at once. [long pause] Finger by finger. [long pause] Feel the tendons release. [long pause] Feel the palms soften. [long pause] Feel the shift that happens in your shoulders, your chest, your belly as the grip lets go.

Open your hands all the way. [long pause] Turn your palms upward if you can. [long pause] Let them rest on your knees or at your sides, face up. [long pause] Open. [long pause] Empty. [long pause] Receiving.

Feel the difference. [long pause] The open hand and the clenched fist are not just different postures — they are different ways of being in the world. [long pause] One says: "I have to hold this together." The other says: "I am held."

Jesus said: "Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear. [long pause] Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothes? [long pause] Can any one of you by worrying add a single hour to your life?" Matthew six, twenty-five through twenty-seven.
Can any one of you by worrying add a single hour to your life?

The answer is no. [long pause] We know this. [long pause] And yet we keep worrying — because worry feels like doing something. [long pause] It feels productive. [long pause] It tricks us into thinking that if we just think about it hard enough, anticipate every possible failure, run every scenario... [long pause] [long pause] we can prevent the bad thing from happening.

We can't. [long pause] Control is an illusion. [long pause] And it costs us everything — our sleep, our peace, our presence, our joy. [long pause] We spend so much energy holding the world together that we have nothing left for actually living in it.
Proverbs three, verses five and six: "Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight."
Let me pray over you now. [long pause] Keep your hands open if you can.

Lord... [long pause] [long pause] we come to you with open hands. [long pause] And that is harder than it sounds. [long pause] Because we have been gripping things so tightly, for so long, that our fingers have forgotten how to straighten.

We bring you the things we've named. [long pause] The outcomes we're trying to force. [long pause] The people we're trying to fix. [long pause] The futures we're trying to guarantee. [long pause] The illusion of control that exhausts us daily.
We don't hand these to you because they don't matter. [long pause] We hand them to you because they matter so much that our grip has become a prison.

Teach us to trust you. [long pause] Not the easy trust of a problem that resolved itself. [long pause] The hard trust of an open hand in an uncertain world. [long pause] The trust that says: I don't know what happens next, but I know who holds it.
"Trust in the Lord with all your heart." We want to. [long pause] Help us where we can't.
Into your hands we place what we cannot carry. [long pause] Into your hands we release what we cannot control. [long pause] Into your hands.
Stay here for a moment. [long pause] Feel your body. [long pause] Feel your open hands. [long pause] Feel the air on your palms.

When you're ready, begin to close your hands slowly. [long pause] Not into fists — into softly curled fingers. [long pause] A relaxed hand. [long pause] Neither gripping nor straining to stay open. [long pause] This is the goal: not permanent surrender, but a hand that knows how to open. [long pause] That remembers the feel of letting go.
Take a slow breath. [long pause] Feel your feet on the floor. [long pause] The chair beneath you. [long pause] The weight of your body, supported.
You are not holding the world together. [long pause] You never were. [long pause] And the One who is... [long pause] [long pause] is not struggling.
Go gently. [long pause] With open hands.
